title: Wire make_did_key into Identity::pub_id() and verify_challenge()
description:
Update two call sites to use make_did_key() instead of hash_key().

What: One-line change in each of identity.rs and auth.rs.

Algorithm:
1. In core/src/identity.rs line 94: change
   PubId(hash_key(self.signing_key.verifying_key().as_bytes()))
   to:
   PubId(make_did_key(self.signing_key.verifying_key().as_bytes()))
2. In core/src/auth.rs: find verify_challenge() — change
   Ok(PubId(hash_key(key.as_bytes())))
   to:
   Ok(PubId(make_did_key(key.as_bytes())))
   (note: make_did_key is in identity.rs; auth.rs imports from agora_core::identity or similar — adjust use statement)
3. Remove use of hash_key() in both files (will become dead code)
4. Update PubId struct doc comment (lines 22-27 of identity.rs) to describe did:key format instead of SHA-256

Defensive programming:
- verify_challenge() invariant must be preserved: PubId is only derived AFTER signature verification
- No unwrap() without comment
- PubId is a public identifier, not secret — safe to log/display

Agent team: standard (R + I + T)

Tests (in identity.rs):
- pub_id_has_did_key_format: generate Identity, assert pub_id().0.starts_with("did:key:z"), assert validate_did_key(&pub_id().0).is_ok()
- pub_id_consistent_with_verify_challenge: no code change needed to existing export_and_restore test — just verify it still passes
acceptance_criteria: cargo test -p agora-core passes; cargo check -p agora-core clean; hash_key() is no longer called (dead code warning or removal)
